kubectl -n $NS get secret rabbitmq-app -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d; echo

content.text: App-user has empty `configure` permission on `/app`, so it can't declare the queue. Fixing that permission.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-tls-rotation-20260710-b7461b9a-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /app app-user ".*" ".*" ".*"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /app

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-tls-rotation-20260710-b7461b9a-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS delete pod -l app=app-producer --ignore-not-found
sleep 15
kubectl -n $NS get pods
echo "=== producer logs ==="
kubectl -n $NS logs deploy/app-producer --tail=50
